70	// beadAutoCommit commits .ddx/beads.jsonl if git.auto_commit is "always".
71	// The operation string describes what happened (e.g. "create ddx-abc123").
72	// Errors are silently ignored — auto-commit is best-effort.
73	// When a commit lands, the resulting SHA is returned.
74	func (f *CommandFactory) beadAutoCommit(operation string) string {
75		workspaceRoot := f.beadWorkspaceRoot()
76		if workspaceRoot == "" {
77			workspaceRoot = f.WorkingDir
78		}
79	
80		cfg, err := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(workspaceRoot)
81		if err != nil {
82			return ""
83		}
84		if cfg.Git == nil {
85			return ""
86		}
87		acCfg := gitpkg.AutoCommitConfig{
88			AutoCommit:   cfg.Git.AutoCommit,
89			CommitPrefix: cfg.Git.CommitPrefix,
90		}
91		beadsFile := filepath.Join(workspaceRoot, ".ddx", "beads.jsonl")
92		sha, _ := gitpkg.AutoCommit(beadsFile, "beads", operation, acCfg)
93		return sha
94	}

1	// Package bead implements the on-disk bead tracker.
2	//
3	// Size envelope (ddx-f8a11202):
4	//
5	// Individual bead fields — description, acceptance, notes, and each event's
6	// body — are bounded by MaxFieldBytes (65,535 bytes). This matches upstream
7	// bd's Dolt TEXT column limit so DDx-authored beads can always round-trip
8	// through `bd import`. Writers exceeding the cap are truncated with a
9	// `…[truncated, N bytes]` marker at AppendEvent; callers that need to
10	// preserve the full payload (notably reviewer streams) should persist to a
11	// sidecar artifact under `.ddx/executions/<id>/` and record a path
12	// reference in the event body.
13	//
14	// On the read side, scanners use a 16MB buffer — real-world incidents have
15	// produced 1.46MB bead rows and ~7MB session-log rows when writers bypassed
16	// the cap. 16MB comfortably fits a bead with dozens of maxed-out fields and
17	// matches the scanner in the agent package.

39	// Store manages beads via a pluggable backend.
40	type Store struct {
41		Collection string
42		Dir        string
43		File       string
44		Prefix     string
45		LockDir    string
46		LockWait   time.Duration
47		backend    Backend // nil means use built-in JSONL
48	}
49	
50	type StoreOption func(*Store)
51	
52	// WithCollection selects the logical bead collection. The default collection
53	// remains "beads", which maps to "beads.jsonl" in the JSONL backend.
54	func WithCollection(name string) StoreOption {
55		return func(s *Store) {
56			if cleaned := normalizeCollection(name); cleaned != "" {
57				s.Collection = cleaned
58			}
59		}
60	}
61	
62	// NewStore creates a store with the given directory.
63	// Defaults can be overridden via options or environment.
64	func NewStore(dir string, opts ...StoreOption) *Store {
65		if dir == "" {
66			dir = envOr("DDX_BEAD_DIR", ".ddx")
67		}
68		workingDir := dir
69		if filepath.Base(dir) == ".ddx" {
70			workingDir = filepath.Dir(dir)
71		}
72		prefix := envOr("DDX_BEAD_PREFIX", "")
73		if prefix == "" {
74			if cfg, err := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(workingDir); err == nil && cfg != nil && cfg.Bead != nil && cfg.Bead.IDPrefix != "" {
75				prefix = cfg.Bead.IDPrefix
76			}
77		}
78		if prefix == "" {
79			prefix = detectPrefix(workingDir)
80		}

36	func TestArchiveMovesEligibleClosedBeads(t *testing.T) {
37		s, dir := archiveTestStore(t)
38		old := time.Now().UTC().Add(-60 * 24 * time.Hour)
39		beads := []Bead{
40			closedBeadAt("ddx-old1", "old closed 1", old),
41			closedBeadAt("ddx-old2", "old closed 2", old),
42			{
43				ID:        "ddx-fresh",
44				Title:     "fresh closed",
45				Status:    StatusClosed,
46				Priority:  2,
47				IssueType: DefaultType,
48				CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC().Add(-time.Hour),
49				UpdatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
50			},
51			{
52				ID:        "ddx-open",
53				Title:     "still open",
54				Status:    StatusOpen,
55				Priority:  2,
56				IssueType: DefaultType,
57				CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC().Add(-time.Hour),
58				UpdatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
59			},
60		}
61		require.NoError(t, s.WriteAll(beads))
62	
63		policy := DefaultArchivePolicy()
64		policy.MinActiveCount = 0
65		moved, err := s.Archive(policy)
66		require.NoError(t, err)
67		assert.ElementsMatch(t, []string{"ddx-old1", "ddx-old2"}, moved)
68	
69		// AC1: archive file is written.
70		archivePath := filepath.Join(dir, BeadsArchiveCollection+".jsonl")
71		info, err := os.Stat(archivePath)
72		require.NoError(t, err, "beads-archive.jsonl must exist")
73		assert.Greater(t, info.Size(), int64(0))
74	
75		// Active retains only the fresh-closed and open beads.
76		remaining, err := s.ReadAll()
77		require.NoError(t, err)
78		ids := make([]string, 0, len(remaining))
79		for _, b := range remaining {
80			ids = append(ids, b.ID)
81		}
82		assert.ElementsMatch(t, []string{"ddx-fresh", "ddx-open"}, ids)
83	
84		// Archive contains the moved beads with archived_at set.
85		archive := s.archivePartner()
86		archived, err := archive.ReadAll()
87		require.NoError(t, err)
88		assert.Len(t, archived, 2)
89		for _, b := range archived {
90			stamp, ok := b.Extra["archived_at"].(string)
91			assert.True(t, ok, "archived bead %s should carry archived_at", b.ID)

14	// TestAttachmentClosingExternalizesEvents asserts that closing a bead moves
15	// its inline event history into a sidecar attachment under
16	// .ddx/attachments/<id>/events.jsonl and clears the inline copy from the row.
17	//
18	// Acceptance: AC1 (closed beads write events to the attachment store, not
19	// inline) and AC3 (bead-row size shrinks).
20	func TestAttachmentClosingExternalizesEvents(t *testing.T) {
21		s := newTestStore(t)
22	
23		b := &Bead{Title: "to close"}
24		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
25		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "review", Summary: "APPROVE", Body: "looks good"}))
26		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "summary", Summary: "completed"}))
27	
28		// Capture the row size before close so we can demonstrate AC3 shrinkage.
29		beforeRowBytes := len(beadRowBytes(t, s, b.ID))
30	
31		require.NoError(t, s.Close(b.ID))
32	
33		// Sidecar exists.
34		attPath := s.eventsAttachmentPath(b.ID)
35		info, err := os.Stat(attPath)
36		require.NoError(t, err, "events.jsonl sidecar should exist after close")
37		assert.True(t, info.Size() > 0)
38	
39		// The on-disk row no longer carries inline events; it carries an
40		// attachment ref instead.
41		rowBytes := beadRowBytes(t, s, b.ID)
42		assert.NotContains(t, string(rowBytes), `"events":`)
43		assert.Contains(t, string(rowBytes), `"`+EventsAttachmentExtraKey+`":`)
44	
45		// AC3: the row shrank.
46		assert.Less(t, len(rowBytes), beforeRowBytes,
47			"closed bead row should be smaller after externalization")
48	
49		// Sidecar carries both events with their bodies intact.
50		events, err := s.readEventsAttachment(b.ID)
51		require.NoError(t, err)

package cmd

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestMigrateCommand(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)

	// Seed two closed beads (one with inline events, one without) plus an
	// open bead, then run `ddx bead migrate`.
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx"), 0o755))
	old := time.Now().UTC().Add(-90 * 24 * time.Hour).Format(time.RFC3339)
	rows := strings.Join([]string{
		`{"id":"ddx-c1","title":"closed with events","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"` + old + `","updated_at":"` + old + `","closing_commit_sha":"deadbeef","events":[{"kind":"review","summary":"APPROVE","body":"ok","created_at":"` + old + `"}]}`,
		`{"id":"ddx-c2","title":"closed no events","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"` + old + `","updated_at":"` + old + `","closing_commit_sha":"deadbeef"}`,
		`{"id":"ddx-open","title":"open","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"` + old + `","updated_at":"` + old + `"}`,
	}, "\n") + "\n"
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx", "beads.jsonl"), []byte(rows), 0o644))

	beforeStatus, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "bead", "status", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	out, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "bead", "migrate", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	var stats struct {
		EventsExternalized int `json:"EventsExternalized"`
		Archived           int `json:"Archived"`
	}
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &stats))
	assert.Equal(t, 1, stats.EventsExternalized)
	assert.Equal(t, 2, stats.Archived)

	// AC2: archive + attachments populated.
	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx", "beads-archive.jsonl"))
	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx", "attachments", "ddx-c1", "events.jsonl"))

	// AC3: status totals identical pre/post.
	afterStatus, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "bead", "status", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, beforeStatus, afterStatus)

	// AC4: show works for archived bead.
	showOut, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "bead", "show", "ddx-c1", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	var shown map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(showOut), &shown))
	assert.Equal(t, "ddx-c1", shown["id"])

	// AC4: list (default) still includes the archived bead.
	listOut, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "bead", "list", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	var listed []map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(listOut), &listed))
	ids := map[string]bool{}
	for _, b := range listed {
		ids[b["id"].(string)] = true
	}
	assert.True(t, ids["ddx-c1"], "archived bead should appear in default list")
	assert.True(t, ids["ddx-c2"], "archived bead should appear in default list")
	assert.True(t, ids["ddx-open"], "open bead should still appear")

	// AC6: idempotency — second pass no-ops.
	out2, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "bead", "migrate", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	var stats2 struct {
		EventsExternalized int `json:"EventsExternalized"`
		Archived           int `json:"Archived"`
	}
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out2), &stats2))
	assert.Equal(t, 0, stats2.EventsExternalized)
	assert.Equal(t, 0, stats2.Archived)
}

Exit status 0; no warnings.

## AC2 — beads.jsonl < 1MB, archive + attachments populated

```
$ du -h .ddx/beads.jsonl .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl
24K     .ddx/beads.jsonl
2.6M    .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl
$ ls .ddx/attachments/ | wc -l
559
```

Active shrank 5.2M → 24K (well below 1MB ceiling).

## AC3 — `ddx bead status` totals identical before and after

```
$ ddx bead status --json   # before
{"open":10,"closed":1161,"blocked":7,"ready":3,"total":1172}
$ ddx bead migrate ...
$ ddx bead status --json   # after
{"open":10,"closed":1161,"blocked":7,"ready":3,"total":1172}
```

Identical. `Store.Status()` was updated to read both active and
archive collections (deduped by ID).

## AC4 — show/list/ready/blocked/dep tree return identical results

* `bead show` already used `GetWithArchive`; archived beads are
  addressable.
* `bead list` (default) now consults `ListWithArchive` so archived
  closed beads still appear; `--all` retained as a no-op.
* `Ready`/`Blocked` only ever surface open beads, and `Archive()`'s
  preserve_dependencies guard keeps any closed bead an open one
  references in the active collection — verified by
  `TestMigratePreservesReferencedDeps`.
* `DepTree` updated to merge active + archive so the tree
  topology is unchanged after migration.

## AC5 — `cd cli && go test -run TestMigrate ./cmd/ ./internal/bead/`

```
$ go test -run TestMigrate ./cmd/ ./internal/bead/
ok  	github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/cmd	0.065s
ok  	github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead	0.038s
```

Tests added:

* `internal/bead/migrate_test.go`:
  - `TestMigrateExternalizesAndArchives`
  - `TestMigrateIsIdempotent`
  - `TestMigratePreservesData`
  - `TestMigratePreservesReferencedDeps`
* `cmd/bead_migrate_test.go`:
  - `TestMigrateCommand` (end-to-end via cobra)

Full `internal/bead` suite still passes:
```
$ go test ./internal/bead/
ok  	github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead	4.588s
```

## AC6 — idempotent

```
$ ddx bead migrate
Externalized events: 0
Archived beads:      0
No changes — already migrated.
```

`Migrate()` only externalizes when there are inline events to move and
only archives via `Archive()` (which is a no-op when nothing is
eligible). `TestMigrateIsIdempotent` byte-compares both files between
two consecutive passes.
